km.Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland: អេយរ, អេយរស៊ីរe, ស្កុតឡេន

zh.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie於1338年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾艾爾。她和Sir David Currie至少有1個兒子。她去世於1370年在她的家鄉享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie于1338年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾艾爾。她和Sir David Currie至少有1个儿子。她去世于1370年在她的家乡享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: Currie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Currie in Midlothian, first recorded in this form in 1230. It is derived from Gaelic curraigh, dative case of currach ‘wet plain, marsh’.
Scottish: habitational name from Corrie in Dumfriesshire or another place so named (see   Corrie  ).
Scottish form of Irish Curry or, in Arran, an Anglicized form of Mac Mhuirich (see McMurray ).

variants.name: Corrie
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of the places in Arran, Dumfries, and elsewhere, named Corrie, from Gaelic coire ‘cauldron’, applied to a circular hanging valley on a mountain. The Corrie family, on record from the late 12th cent., were tenants of the Bruce lordship of Annandale in the Middle Ages.
Scottish: shortened form of   McCorry   ‘son of Godfrey’. See also   Curry  ,   McGorry  .
English and Scottish: topographic name denoting someone who lived ‘(at the) shepherd's hut’, Middle English cori. Alternatively, a variant of Currey .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie was born in 1338, in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. She had at least 1 son with Sir David Currie. She died in 1370, in her hometown, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie est née en 1338, à Ayr, Ayrshire, Écosse. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir David Currie. Elle est décédée en 1370, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie wurde 1338 in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir David Currie mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1370 im Alter von 32 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie è nata il nel 1338 nel Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir David Currie. Morì nel 1370, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland: スコットランドエイルシュアイアエイル
ja.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currieは1338年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアエイルで生まれた。彼女はSir David Currieとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1370年に故郷で32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie(은)는 1338년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 에이르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir David Currie(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1370년 그녀의 고향에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie nasceu em 1338, em Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir David Currie. Ela faleceu em 1370, em sua cidade natal, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie родилась в 1338 г. в местности аир, аиршир, Шотландия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir David Currie. Она умерла в 1370 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 32 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Currie nació en 1338, en Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir David Currie. Murió en 1370, en su pueblo, a la edad de 32 años.
